speaker: interviewer
text: This is tape two, the interview with Jeremiah Kirschenbaum.

speaker: interviewer
text: The date is December 16th, 1996. You were telling me about Baranow, and how you lived there when you first came there.

speaker: interviewee
text: We came to Baranow because we-- we can-- we cannot live in Padew. Why? For the German wanted to-- to take together all the Jewish in one place. We came to Baranow a few weeks. After this, the Judenrat-- the German give a-- a-- a-- a time. And he wants to work 200 young-- young people. And the Judenrat took us.

speaker: interviewer
text: Before you left Padew, did you take things with you? Did you take pots? Did you-- you told me you took some things with you from Padew to Baranow. Did the Germans or the Poles come and take your things away in Padew? Did they come to your house before you left Padew? How did you leave your house when you left Padew?

speaker: interviewee
text: We leaved everything in house. What we can take on the shoulders, we took. That's all.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you remember what you took?

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you remember what you took?

speaker: interviewee
text: Most things to eat. That's all. A little bit clothing, maybe.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did you try to hide any valuables like--

speaker: interviewee
text: [LAUGHS] This-- maybe today it's someplace. Nobody won't know where to-- to is.

speaker: interviewee
text: What we got? We got no big-- silver things, that's all.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did you try to come back for it after the war?

speaker: interviewee
text: No. No. Yeah, I was-- after the war-- I was in Mielec. This is later.

speaker: interviewer
text: We'll talk about Mielec a little bit later. Let's continue now with with Baranow. Did-- how many people came to Baranow the same time you did? Do you know? Were there a lot of people? Or a few people?

speaker: interviewee
text: I-- I was in-- in in Padew. [PAUSES FOR 7 SECONDS] No, this was after the war. After the war.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where did they take you when they took you from Baranow? From Baranow, the Germans, when they took you? The Judenrat-- when the Judenrat--

speaker: interviewee
text: I went to-- to Huta Komorowska to be in the logging, yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: How far from Baranow was it?

speaker: interviewee
text: Maybe-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] maybe to-- to travel, 5 hours.

speaker: interviewer
text: How many people were with you when they took you? How many people did they take with you? [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: It was, from Baranow in Huta Komorowska this was maybe 100 people, more.

speaker: interviewer
text: And did they-- did they tell you where they were taking you?

speaker: interviewer
text: When they took you from-- from Baranow to Huta.

speaker: interviewee
text: We know where we going to. It's in this logging.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did they tell you what you were going to do?

speaker: interviewer
text: Did they tell you what you were gonna do?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, no. This-- no. We-- we worked by-- by trees.

speaker: interviewee
text: To take the-- the wood. The German put to Germany. But Germany got no-- no-- no-- no wood.

speaker: interviewer
text: How many-- how did they travel-- make you travel? Did they take you in buses, in trucks? How did they take you?

speaker: interviewee
text: We-- we go-- we go with-- with the army goes. Together.

speaker: interviewer
text: How long did it take? [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: And where did they keep you when they got you there?

speaker: interviewer
text: Where did they put you when they brought you to that place?

speaker: interviewee
text: That place? We was on outside. We worked by trees.

speaker: interviewer
text: Did they take you back at the end of the day?

speaker: interviewee
text: In the nighttime we went back to-- to--

speaker: interviewee
text: Not to Baranow-- to Baranow-- to Baranow. No, no, to the Huta Komorowska, they did this.

speaker: interviewer
text: How long were you in Huta Komorowska?

speaker: interviewee
text: I was in this-- in this concentration camp by [INAUDIBLE] summer 1943.

speaker: interviewer
text: What did you do in that concentration camp?

speaker: interviewee
text: No, we worked by the-- with the wood.

speaker: interviewer
text: What kind of work did you--

speaker: interviewee
text: Not, you got in a different city, too. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH], yeah. A lot working in the kitchen to-- to cook. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Water and-- and a potato when you can find.

speaker: interviewee
text: I don't remember. I was in three-- three up [INAUDIBLE]. [PAUSES FOR 8 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. [PAUSES FOR 24 SECONDS] Oh yeah. One-- I think my wife maybe said this. No, I don't remember the number. Oh, was killed, too, in the street.

speaker: interviewee
text: [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. I don't-- I-- I no remember [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].

speaker: interviewee
text: [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. [PAUSES FOR 9 SECONDS] In summer 1944, I went to Mielec.

speaker: interviewer
text: Mielec. How did they take you to Mielec? [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]?

speaker: interviewer
text: [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]? Did they make a selection?

speaker: interviewee
text: [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. I don't know the reason why it was in the nighttime. [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewee
text: [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH]. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS]

speaker: interviewer
text: [NON-ENGLISH] break. We will take a tape change here and make a break.
